instruction: Insert a blank table with 6 columns and half the number of rows as the columns in the body text box of the last slide.
provide_info:
Slide 1:
Food Price Outlook
Matthew MacLachlan
USDA - Economic Research Service
2022 USDA Agricultural Outlook Forum
February 24th – 25th, 2022

Slide 2:
Outline
Overview of consumer spending on food
Food price trends over time
Food prices in 2021
2022 food price forecasts in (recent) historical context
Next Food Price Outlook release
Additional ERS Publications

Slide 3:
Most information available on ERS website
Food Price Outlook Monthly Report
Interactive visualizations
Charting the Essentials
Charts of Note

Slide 4:
U.S. consumers spent about 12% of expenditures on food in 2020
Based on BLS Consumer Expenditure Survey Data

Slide 5:
Food expenditure share (~12%) similar to historical average

Slide 6:
Food price inflation over past 5 years:
Below housing and transportation
Slightly above overall inflation

Slide 7:
Consumer spending differs across food categories
CPI weighting reflects these differences

Slide 8:
ERS reports detailed food subcategories

Slide 9:
Food at home vs. food away from home:
Different price growth patterns
More similar during pandemic

Slide 10:
Retail food price changes in 2021
Significant increases observed

Slide 11:
Examples of 2021 price increases:
+9.3%
+8.6%

Slide 12:
Some categories had smaller increases (e.g., ~1.1%)

Slide 13:
Key factors for 2022:
Pandemic-related changes
Variants and vaccination
Supply chains
International trade
U.S. economy

Slide 14:
2022 food price inflation forecast:
2% – 3%

Slide 15:
Meat prices:
Sharp increase early pandemic
Another rise in late 2021

Slide 16:
Food away from home:
Faster price growth than usual
Forecast increase: 3.5% – 4.5% (2022)

Slide 17:
Takeaways:
2021:
High food price inflation
Meat prices increased most
All categories increased (vegetables least)
2022:
Continued uncertainty
Food prices +2–3%
Food away from home +3.5–4.5%

Slide 18:
Future updates:
Food Price Outlook updated regularly
Covers current and next year forecasts
Includes farm, wholesale, retail categories

Slide 19:
ERS additional datasets:
Food Dollar Series
Food Expenditure Series
Farm-to-consumer price spreads

Slide 20:
Additional ERS resources:
Amber Waves
Charts of Note
Charting the Essentials
Email subscription available

Slide 21:
Thank you
Matthew MacLachlan
USDA Economic Research Service
Contact: Matthew.MacLachlan@usda.gov :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
